summary: Phenomenal sound captures a nostalgic Las Vegas show highlighted by Unbroken Chain, Jack Straw and So Many Roads.
review: The recording delivers clear, lively audio that lets listeners relive a hot‑day Vegas concert where the band rolled out a memorable opener with Jack Straw and a second‑set highlight featuring Unbroken Chain and an inspired So Many Roads. While Jerry was noted as tired, his guitar work and vocal endings still shone, and the jam sequence (Corrina > Drums > Outer Space > Area 51 > Spanish Jam) earned particular praise. Overall the performance is mixed‑positive – many fans cherish it as a “last show” memory despite occasional fatigue.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the music and the sound quality, citing standout songs and vivid memories; only a few note tired playing or minor disappointment, yielding an overall rating just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers call the sound phenomenal, crisp and “great”; no major drop‑outs or muddiness are reported. The Corrina’s echo effect is highlighted as especially vivid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong, upbeat opening with Jack Straw set the tone; the second set delivered a highlight in Unbroken Chain and an extended jam (Corrina > Drums > Outer Space > Area 51 > Spanish Jam) that many fans remember fondly despite some fatigue on stage.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as tired but still delivering strong vocals and guitar work; his ending on So Many Roads received particular praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for playing two songs and contributing solid bass to Unbroken Chain.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's high‑pitched keyboard voice was mentioned as detracting from some harmonies.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming highlighted in the Corrina > Drums > Outer Space sequence; a security guard even photographed the drummer on stage.
